sanskrit:
किरीटिनं गदिनं चक्रिणं च तेजोराशिं सर्वतो दीप्तिमन्तम्।
पश्यामि त्वां दुर्निरीक्ष्यं समन्ताद्दीप्तानलार्कद्युतिमप्रमेयम्॥
transliteration:
kirīṭinaṃ gadinaṃ cakriṇaṃ ca tejorāśiṃ sarvato dīptimantam |
paśyāmi tvāṃ durnirīkṣyaṃ samantāddīptānalārka-dyutimaprameyam ||
literal_meaning: With helmet, with mace, with discus, and a mass of effulgence glowing on all sides—I see You, hard to look at, blazing fire-sun-radiance, immeasurable.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna confronts an overwhelming archetype: the luminous Warrior-God whose brilliance is so intense it threatens to obliterate ordinary perception. The psyche is shown an image of total power that simultaneously attracts and blinds, forcing recognition of limits in bearing ultimate truth.
psychological_state: Acute numinous experience: ego dissolving before an archetype of overwhelming light and power, producing both ecstatic attraction and existential dread.
trigger_scenario: Standing before a vast wildfire at night—heat so intense you must turn away, yet feel compelled to stare, realizing your complete smallness against a force that could erase you in seconds.
anchor_text: Face overwhelming truth; let its blaze burn away illusion.
